name: u2X
description: 1U Intellijel dual buffered multiple. Each section copies one input to three active outputs. IN 1 is normalled to section 2, giving up to 6 copies of a single signal. Bicolor LED per section shows polarity.

controls.name: IN 1 LED
controls.description: Bicolor LED showing polarity and level of the signal at IN 1.
controls.detail: green = positive · red = negative · brightness tracks amplitude

controls.name: IN 2 LED
controls.description: Bicolor LED showing polarity and level of the signal at IN 2 (or IN 1 when IN 2 is unpatched).
controls.detail: green = positive · red = negative · brightness tracks amplitude

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Input to section 1. Buffered and copied to outputs 1x1, 1x2, 1x3. Also normalled down to section 2's input when IN 2 is unpatched.

inputs.name: IN 2
inputs.description: Input to section 2. Buffered and copied to outputs 2x1, 2x2, 2x3. Normalled to IN 1 when unpatched.

outputs.name: 2x1
outputs.description: Buffered copy of IN 2 (or IN 1 when IN 2 is unpatched).

outputs.name: 2x2
outputs.description: Buffered copy of IN 2 (or IN 1 when IN 2 is unpatched).

outputs.name: 2x3
outputs.description: Buffered copy of IN 2 (or IN 1 when IN 2 is unpatched).

behaviors.name: Buffered Copy
behaviors.description: Active op-amp buffer drives three identical outputs per section. Unlike a passive mult, the buffer prevents V/Oct sag and impedance loading when copies feed multiple oscillators or high-impedance destinations.

behaviors.name: 1-to-6 Mult
behaviors.description: IN 1 is normalled to IN 2, so one input produces six identical buffered copies across all outputs (1x1, 1x2, 1x3, 2x1, 2x2, 2x3).

behaviors.name: Two Independent Mults
behaviors.description: Patching IN 2 breaks the normal. You then have two independent 1-to-3 buffered mults in 12HP / 1U.

behaviors.name: Polarity Monitor
behaviors.description: Bicolor LED lights green on positive voltage, red on negative. Useful for confirming CV is arriving and for distinguishing unipolar envelopes from bipolar LFOs at a glance.

patchIdeas.name: Precision V/Oct fan-out to three oscillators
patchIdeas.patch: Keyboard V/Oct → IN 1 · outputs 1x1, 1x2, 1x3 → three VCO V/Oct inputs.
steps: Patch your pitch source (keyboard, sequencer, quantizer) into u2X IN 1.
Tune each VCO by ear or use its own fine-tune knob — the buffered outputs will track cleanly across octaves without the pitch droop a passive mult can cause.
patchIdeas.signalOut: 1x1, 1x2, 1x3 — three identical buffered copies of the V/Oct pitch CV, each stable enough to drive a VCO without tuning sag.
patchIdeas.hear: All three oscillators hold tuning together even when you play several octaves. If you swap this for a passive stackcable, the oscillators will drift flat by tens of cents as more loads are added — that's exactly what the buffer is preventing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
u2X:
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> u2X (IN 1)
- u2X (1x1) p> VCO A (V/Oct)
- u2X (1x2) p> VCO B (V/Oct)
- u2X (1x3) p> VCO C (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: One clock, six destinations
patchIdeas.patch: Clock → IN 1 · leave IN 2 unpatched so all six outputs carry the same clock.
steps: Patch master clock (or a square LFO) into u2X IN 1.
Leave IN 2 unpatched — IN 1 is normalled down to section 2, so 2x1/2x2/2x3 also carry the clock.
Fan out: 1x1 → sequencer clock, 1x2 → drum trig, 1x3 → sample-and-hold trig, 2x1 → envelope gate, 2x2 → clocked delay, 2x3 → visual trigger / tap.
Confirm the LED on section 1 is blinking green in time — section 2's LED will blink in sync because it's receiving the same normalled signal.
patchIdeas.signalOut: 1x1, 1x2, 1x3, 2x1, 2x2, 2x3 — six buffered copies of the same clock/trig, ready to drive anything that needs a tick.
patchIdeas.hear: Everything downstream stays locked to the same master tick. Because each output is actively buffered, fast triggers stay sharp even when feeding many modules — no slew rounding or missed gates from loading.
patchIdeas.diagram:
u2X:
- Clock (Out) t> u2X (IN 1)
// IN 2 unpatched — IN 1 normalled to section 2
- u2X (1x1) t> Sequencer (Clock)
- u2X (1x2) t> Drum (Trig)
- u2X (1x3) t> S&H (Trig)
- u2X (2x1) t> Envelope (Gate)
- u2X (2x2) t> Delay (Clock)
- u2X (2x3) t> Scope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Two mod buses in parallel
patchIdeas.patch: LFO → IN 1 for slow bus · envelope → IN 2 for fast bus · route copies to different destinations.
steps: Patch a slow LFO into IN 1 — this becomes your slow modulation bus.
Patch an envelope into IN 2 — this breaks the normal and becomes your fast modulation bus.
From section 1: 1x1 → filter cutoff, 1x2 → VCA bias, 1x3 → panner — all wobble with the LFO.
From section 2: 2x1 → filter resonance, 2x2 → FM index VCA, 2x3 → wavefolder — all punch with the envelope.
Watch the LEDs: section 1 should slowly fade green/red with the LFO; section 2 flashes green on each note.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Section 1 outputs carry the LFO (bipolar, slow). Section 2 outputs carry the envelope (unipolar, fast). Two independent modulation buses, three taps each.
patchIdeas.hear: A patch where slow, wide motion (LFO on filter/VCA/pan) coexists with sharp per-note accents (envelope on resonance/FM/fold). The two buses never cross-talk because the sections are fully independent once IN 2 is patched.
patchIdeas.diagram:
u2X:
- LFO (Out) >> u2X (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> u2X (IN 2)
- u2X (1x1) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- u2X (1x2) >> VCA (Bias)
- u2X (1x3) >> Panner (CV)
- u2X (2x1) >> VCF (Resonance)
- u2X (2x2) >> FM VCA (CV)
- u2X (2x3) >> Wavefolder (CV)
